task_completion.description: Log into the Floreant POS Back Office
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Floreant POS application is open and the Back Office dashboard is visible, indicating successful login (likely using PIN 1111).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the menu item creation screen
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'New Menu Item' dialog, 'Menu Item Explorer', or similar configuration window is open within the Floreant POS interface.
The text field for the menu item name contains exactly 'Surf and Turf'.
The text field for the price (or base price) contains '45.00', '45.0', or '45'.
The inventory tracking, stock, or quantity field contains the number '12'.
The 'Surf and Turf' item is visible in the list of saved menu items, or the creation dialog is shown being successfully saved/submitted.

integrity.description: Agent used the Floreant POS GUI instead of direct database manipulation
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Floreant POS Java application interface, rather than terminal commands injecting SQL into the Derby database.

integrity.description: Agent did not type answers into a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs ('Surf and Turf', '45.00', '12') are entered into the actual Floreant POS form fields, not a notepad, text editor, or unrelated application.
Screenshots show a logical progression of steps (e.g., login screen, back office dashboard, menu configuration) rather than jumping directly to a single static mocked image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target menu item name is exactly 'Surf and Turf' (case-insensitive). The target price is $45.00 (or 45.0). The target initial stock quantity is exactly 12. The agent must perform these actions within the Floreant POS application's Back Office.
